name: Garmin Forerunner 265
summary: A premium running GPS watch with a vivid AMOLED display, HRV Status, Training Readiness, and up to 15 days of battery life in smartwatch mode — designed for serious runners who don't want to compromise on training insights.
features: Running dynamics (cadence, stride length, ground contact time)
Advanced running metrics: Running Power, PacePro
batteryLife: Up to 15 days (smartwatch mode); up to 20 hours (GPS mode)
pros: AMOLED display is significantly sharper and more readable than MIP displays on older Forerunners
HRV Status and Training Readiness give actionable recovery guidance
15-day battery life in smartwatch mode is exceptional for an AMOLED watch
Daily Suggested Workouts adapt to fitness and recovery in real time
ANT+ compatibility allows pairing with foot pods, cycling power meters, and chest straps
cons: No built-in maps or turn-by-turn navigation (step up to Forerunner 965 for that)
AMOLED display drains battery faster during GPS activities vs. MIP competitors
Garmin Connect app is feature-rich but has a steep learning curve
Lacks multi-band GPS of higher-end Garmin models
bestFor: Runners who want long battery without sacrificing display quality
notBestFor: Hikers or adventure users who need topographic maps
Users primarily interested in smartwatch features over training metrics
